summary: Azure compute: virtual machines, scale sets, managed disks, and availability sets. Provisioning a new virtual machine or managed disk is engineer-reviewed; tag updates are self-service; resizing a virtual machine is guardrailed and briefly restarts it; scale-set instance count, disk growth, and disk storage-type changes are guardrailed.

operations.title: Update tags on a Linux virtual machine
operations.summary: Set or update this virtual machine's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the virtual machine via the `tags` map. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on the running workload.

params.name: virtual_machine
params.label: Virtual machine
params.help: The Linux virtual machine to tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed host can be targeted.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Update tags on a Windows virtual machine
operations.summary: Set or update this virtual machine's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the virtual machine via the `tags` map. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on the running workload.

params.name: virtual_machine
params.label: Virtual machine
params.help: The Windows virtual machine to tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed host can be targeted.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Resize a Linux virtual machine
operations.summary: Resize this virtual machine to an approved size. Applying deallocates and restarts it, so expect downtime.
operations.description: Change the `size` argument — the Azure VM SKU that fixes vCPU and memory — to an allowlisted value. Applied in place; Terraform does not replace the virtual machine, but Azure deallocates and restarts it to move it onto the new size, so the workload is briefly unavailable.

params.name: virtual_machine
params.label: Virtual machine
params.help: Select the virtual machine to resize; the picker only lists virtual machines already tracked in inventory, so the change cannot target an unmanaged host.

params.name: size
params.label: New size
params.help: The Azure VM size (SKU) that fixes the host's vCPU and memory; the list is a curated set of common sizes, and applying the change deallocates and restarts the virtual machine.

decisions: Applying this resize deallocates and restarts the virtual machine — schedule it for a maintenance window
Confirm the target size is available in the virtual machine's region and is compatible with its current disk and network configuration before applying

operations.title: Resize a Windows virtual machine
operations.summary: Resize this virtual machine to an approved size. Applying deallocates and restarts it, so expect downtime.
operations.description: Change the `size` argument — the Azure VM SKU that fixes vCPU and memory — to an allowlisted value. Applied in place; Terraform does not replace the virtual machine, but Azure deallocates and restarts it to move it onto the new size, so the workload is briefly unavailable.

params.name: virtual_machine
params.label: Virtual machine
params.help: Select the virtual machine to resize; the picker only lists virtual machines already tracked in inventory, so the change cannot target an unmanaged host.

params.name: size
params.label: New size
params.help: The Azure VM size (SKU) that fixes the host's vCPU and memory; the list is a curated set of common sizes, and applying the change deallocates and restarts the virtual machine.

decisions: Applying this resize deallocates and restarts the virtual machine — schedule it for a maintenance window
Confirm the target size is available in the virtual machine's region and is compatible with its current disk and network configuration before applying

operations.title: Update tags on a Linux scale set
operations.summary: Set or update this scale set's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the virtual machine scale set via the `tags` map. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on running instances.

params.name: scale_set
params.label: Scale set
params.help: The Linux scale set to tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed scale set can be targeted.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Update tags on a Windows scale set
operations.summary: Set or update this scale set's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the virtual machine scale set via the `tags` map. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on running instances.

params.name: scale_set
params.label: Scale set
params.help: The Windows scale set to tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed scale set can be targeted.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Set instance count on a Linux scale set
operations.summary: Scale this scale set to a specific number of instances. Affects running cost and capacity immediately.
operations.description: Change the `instances` argument — the number of virtual machine instances Terraform maintains in the scale set. Applied in place with no resource replacement; Azure adds or removes instances to match. Affects cost and capacity immediately, and scaling down terminates the instances Azure selects for removal.

params.name: scale_set
params.label: Scale set
params.help: Select the Linux scale set to resize; the picker only lists scale sets already tracked in inventory.

params.name: instances
params.label: Instance count
params.help: The number of instances the scale set should run. If an autoscale rule also targets this scale set, that rule's next evaluation can move the count away from what is set here.

decisions: Scaling down chooses which instances Azure terminates — confirm no instance holds irreplaceable local state before applying
If this scale set is also targeted by an autoscale rule, that rule can move the count away from what is set here on its next evaluation

operations.title: Set instance count on a Windows scale set
operations.summary: Scale this scale set to a specific number of instances. Affects running cost and capacity immediately.
operations.description: Change the `instances` argument — the number of virtual machine instances Terraform maintains in the scale set. Applied in place with no resource replacement; Azure adds or removes instances to match. Affects cost and capacity immediately, and scaling down terminates the instances Azure selects for removal.

params.name: scale_set
params.label: Scale set
params.help: Select the Windows scale set to resize; the picker only lists scale sets already tracked in inventory.

params.name: instances
params.label: Instance count
params.help: The number of instances the scale set should run. If an autoscale rule also targets this scale set, that rule's next evaluation can move the count away from what is set here.

decisions: Scaling down chooses which instances Azure terminates — confirm no instance holds irreplaceable local state before applying
If this scale set is also targeted by an autoscale rule, that rule can move the count away from what is set here on its next evaluation

operations.title: Update tags on a managed disk
operations.summary: Set or update this disk's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the managed disk via the `tags` map. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on the attached workload.

params.name: managed_disk
params.label: Managed disk
params.help: The managed disk to tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed disk can be targeted.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Grow a managed disk
operations.summary: Increase this disk's size. Storage can only grow, never shrink, so this can't be undone.
operations.description: Increase a managed disk's size via the `disk_size_gb` argument. Grow-only — an Azure managed disk cannot be shrunk, so this is irreversible.

params.name: managed_disk
params.label: Managed disk
params.help: Select the managed disk to enlarge; the picker lists only estate-managed disks from inventory, and the size field separately enforces grow-only since a managed disk can never be shrunk.

params.name: disk_size_gb
params.label: New size (GiB)
params.help: Must exceed the current size in GiB; a managed disk cannot be shrunk.

decisions: Some virtual machine generations and disk/performance-tier combinations require the virtual machine to be deallocated before a grow takes effect — confirm the target disk supports online resize before applying

operations.title: Change a managed disk's storage type
operations.summary: Change this disk's storage type, e.g. standard HDD to premium SSD. Affects cost and performance; some transitions need it offline.
operations.description: Change the `storage_account_type` argument — the managed disk's storage tier and redundancy combination. Affects cost and performance.

params.name: managed_disk
params.label: Managed disk
params.help: Select the managed disk whose storage type changes; the picker lists only estate-managed disks from inventory.

params.name: storage_account_type
params.label: Storage type
params.help: The disk's storage tier and redundancy. Standard_LRS is cheapest and slowest; Premium_LRS is fastest; the ZRS variants add zone redundancy at extra cost.

decisions: Switching storage tier changes cost and performance — confirm the target tier matches the workload's IOPS/throughput needs

Converting to or from a premium tier on a disk attached to a running virtual machine can require the disk to be detached or the virtual machine deallocated first, depending on VM series — confirm compatibility before applying

operations.summary: Set or update this availability set's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the availability set via the `tags` map. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on member virtual machines.

params.name: tags

operations.title: Provision a new Linux virtual machine
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Linux virtual machine; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new Linux host as a complete specification: size, image approach, network placement, access, OS disk and tags. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block — the virtual machine and its network interface — to review and finish. Nothing is applied without review.

params.name: host_name
params.label: Host name
params.help: Letters, numbers and dashes, starting with a letter, up to 64 characters. Names the virtual machine in Azure and in the generated draft.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group this virtual machine is created in. Its region is reused automatically for the virtual machine and its network interface, so it is never asked twice.

params.name: size
params.label: Size
params.help: The Azure VM size (SKU) that fixes vCPU and memory — the same curated list the resize action offers.

params.name: zone
params.label: Availability zone
params.help: Optional availability zone to pin the virtual machine to; leave blank to let Azure place it automatically.

params.name: image
params.label: Machine image
params.help: The engineer picks and validates the marketplace or gallery image during review — approved images follow the account standard.

params.name: admin_username
params.label: Admin username
params.help: The first sign-in account created on the virtual machine. Avoid reserved names like root or admin — Azure rejects a handful of common ones.

params.name: ssh_public_key
params.label: SSH public key
params.help: The operator never pastes key material here — the engineer attaches the account's approved public key during review.

params.name: disable_password_authentication
params.label: Password sign-in
params.help: Always off for a new Linux host — SSH key sign-in only. Written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: subnet
params.label: Subnet
params.help: Where the virtual machine's network interface lives. The picker shows each subnet's address range.

params.name: nsg
params.label: Network security group
params.help: Who can reach this virtual machine. Attached to its network interface.

params.name: os_disk_size_gb
params.label: OS disk size (GiB)
params.help: OS disk size. Data disks are added afterwards as separate managed disks.

params.name: os_disk_type
params.label: OS disk type
params.help: The OS disk's storage tier — the same list the managed-disk storage-type action offers. Premium_LRS is the recommended default for consistent boot and application performance.

params.name: os_disk_caching
params.label: OS disk caching
params.help: Standard caching mode for an OS disk — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Host name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What runs here
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this host.

decisions: Machine image choice and validation
SSH public key material is attached by the engineer during review
Confirm the subnet and network security group together satisfy the workload's connectivity needs
Confirm the chosen size is available in the resource group's region

operations.title: Provision a new Windows virtual machine
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Windows virtual machine; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new Windows host as a complete specification: size, image approach, network placement, access, OS disk and tags. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block — the virtual machine and its network interface — to review and finish. Nothing is applied without review.

params.name: host_name
params.label: Host name
params.help: Letters, numbers and dashes, starting with a letter, up to 15 characters — Windows derives its in-guest computer name from this, and that name is capped at 15 characters.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group this virtual machine is created in. Its region is reused automatically for the virtual machine and its network interface, so it is never asked twice.

params.name: size
params.label: Size
params.help: The Azure VM size (SKU) that fixes vCPU and memory — the same curated list the resize action offers.

params.name: zone
params.label: Availability zone
params.help: Optional availability zone to pin the virtual machine to; leave blank to let Azure place it automatically.

params.name: image
params.label: Machine image
params.help: The engineer picks and validates the marketplace or gallery image during review — approved images follow the account standard.

params.name: admin_username
params.label: Admin username
params.help: The first sign-in account created on the virtual machine, up to 20 characters. Azure rejects reserved names like administrator or admin.

params.name: admin_password
params.label: Administrator password
params.help: The operator never sets a password here — the engineer sets it during review, per the account's credential-handling standard.

params.name: subnet
params.label: Subnet
params.help: Where the virtual machine's network interface lives. The picker shows each subnet's address range.

params.name: nsg
params.label: Network security group
params.help: Who can reach this virtual machine. Attached to its network interface.

params.name: os_disk_size_gb
params.label: OS disk size (GiB)
params.help: OS disk size. Data disks are added afterwards as separate managed disks.

params.name: os_disk_type
params.label: OS disk type
params.help: The OS disk's storage tier — the same list the managed-disk storage-type action offers. Premium_LRS is the recommended default for consistent boot and application performance.

params.name: os_disk_caching
params.label: OS disk caching
params.help: Standard caching mode for an OS disk — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Host name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What runs here
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this host.

decisions: Machine image choice and validation
Administrator password is set by the engineer during review, per the account's credential-handling standard
Confirm the subnet and network security group together satisfy the workload's connectivity needs
Confirm the chosen size is available in the resource group's region

operations.title: Create a new managed disk
operations.summary: Request a brand-new managed disk; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Create a new Azure managed disk, encrypted at rest by default, on a curated storage tier, with estate tags. Submitting routes the complete specification to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: disk_name
params.label: Disk name
params.help: Letters, numbers, periods, underscores and dashes, 2-80 characters. Names the disk in Azure and in the generated draft.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group this disk is created in. Its region is reused automatically for the disk.

params.name: create_option
params.label: Create option
params.help: Always a blank new disk — written into the draft, never asked. Restoring from a snapshot uses the field below instead.

params.name: size_gb
params.label: Size (GiB)
params.help: Can grow later without downtime; a managed disk can never shrink.

params.name: storage_account_type
params.label: Storage type
params.help: The disk's storage tier and redundancy — the same list the storage-type change action offers. StandardSSD_LRS is a balanced default for general-purpose data disks.

params.name: zone
params.label: Availability zone
params.help: Optional availability zone; match the virtual machine this disk will attach to, or leave blank.

params.name: disk_encryption_set
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Only set this if the workload needs its own customer-managed key; every managed disk is encrypted at rest by Azure regardless, so leaving this blank still means encrypted.

params.name: source_snapshot
params.label: Start from a snapshot
params.help: Restore data by creating the disk from this snapshot. Leave blank for an empty disk.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Disk name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this disk holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this disk.

decisions: Attaching this disk to a virtual machine is a separate, follow-up step
Confirm the disk's zone (if set) matches the virtual machine it will attach to
Confirm the encryption key choice if the workload is key-scoped
